description: Comprehensive plan for recreating the Jarvis/Operator system from scratch, including architecture, phased development, recommended improvements, and historical issue resolution.

frontend.state_management: Unified Context API (VoiceContext) or Zustand for high-frequency data
frontend.core_features: Conversation mode toggle, communication history, wake sensitivity controls, visual tracking indicators

core_services: Decision Engine: Orchestrates logic and LLM interactions
Memory Manager: Handles short-term and long-term context
System Watchers: Monitors file system events and system state
Audio Processing: Local transcription fallback and TTS generation

ai_and_vision_modules.voice_activation: Hybrid Architecture: Web Speech API for zero-latency burst wake-word detection, backed by local Python transcription.

recreation_phases.title: Foundation & Infrastructure Setup
tasks: Initialize Git repository and define strict .gitignore (exclude logs, node_modules, venv).
Setup Python virtual environment and standard requirements.txt.
Scaffold Vite + React application and lock port to 8080 in vite.config.ts.
Implement environment variables (.env) to banish hardcoded credentials and absolute paths.
Create the Silent Launcher (VBScript + launcher.py) ensuring it ignores its own log files.

recreation_phases.title: Core Backend Communications
tasks: Develop the FastAPI backend with a single, unified WebSocket endpoint.
Create foundational Decision Engine and Memory class structures.
Set up System/File Watchers with strict exclude-rules (e.g., ignore *.log) to prevent infinite loops.

recreation_phases.title: Frontend Unified Architecture
tasks: Build a centralized WebSocket provider in React to prevent dual-connection race conditions.
Develop the main dashboard UI (dark-themed, dynamic layout).
Implement the Conversation History view and System Status indicators.

recreation_phases.title: Advanced Voice & Audio Subsystem
tasks: Integrate the Web Speech API for instantaneous wake-word detection.
Connect the Python-based local transcription service as a reliable offline fallback.
Crucially implement `isSpeakingRef` state guards to mute the microphone during TTS playback (prevents the AI from hearing itself).

recreation_phases.title: Vision System Integration
tasks: Implement HandTracker.tsx using CDN-based MediaPipe to bypass asset loading errors.
Configure camera stream quality (brightness, resolution).
Add landmark logging and visual detection status indicators to the UI.

recreation_phases.title: Optimization, Security & Polish
tasks: Audit all async Python code to ensure the main event loop is not blocked by file I/O or AI inference.
Secure all sensitive tokens (Telegram, OpenAI, etc.) in the .env file.
Refine tray controls and ensure graceful shutdown of all subprocesses.

recommended_improvements.details: Ensure heavy processing (LLM calls, heavy I/O) are offloaded to asyncio.to_thread or a dedicated message queue (like Redis/Celery) so the WebSocket server never blocks.
Instead of a monolithic Decision Engine, use a plugin architecture where new skills (Telegram alerts, Smart Home, Web Search) can be dynamically loaded.
Migrate away from complex, deeply nested React Contexts to a dedicated state manager to handle high-frequency data (like Hand Tracking coordinates) without causing massive re-renders.
Implement an algorithm to summarize and prune long-running conversation history in the Memory Manager to prevent LLM token bloat.

historical_issues_and_fixes.symptoms: The microphone picked up the system's own TTS output, causing infinite conversational loops and hallucinations.
historical_issues_and_fixes.fix_implemented: Added strict `isSpeakingRef` state guards in `VoiceContext.tsx` to explicitly pause audio processing while Jarvis is speaking.

historical_issues_and_fixes.symptoms: HandTracker module frequently failed to load due to missing local assets or Vite bundler pathing errors.
historical_issues_and_fixes.fix_implemented: Switched MediaPipe to a CDN-based loading approach in `HandTracker.tsx`, ensuring assets are reliably fetched independent of the local build.

historical_issues_and_fixes.symptoms: Vite would randomly assign new ports if 8080 was briefly occupied, breaking the Tray application's hardcoded dashboard URL.
historical_issues_and_fixes.fix_implemented: Permanently locked the frontend to port 8080 via `vite.config.ts` and synced `backend/tray.py` to always expect this port.

historical_issues_and_fixes.issue: File Watcher Infinite Loop / Telegram Spam
historical_issues_and_fixes.symptoms: The file watcher monitored its own log output. When a log was written, it triggered a Telegram alert, which wrote another log, causing a feedback loop.
historical_issues_and_fixes.fix_implemented: Updated `launcher.py` and watcher configurations to strictly ignore `*.log` files and the application's own output directories.

historical_issues_and_fixes.symptoms: Multiple UI components attempted to open separate WebSocket connections, duplicating messages and crashing the backend.
historical_issues_and_fixes.fix_implemented: Refactored the React frontend to use a single, unified WebSocket provider architecture that shares the connection globally.

historical_issues_and_fixes.symptoms: Sending all audio to the Python backend for wake-word detection caused unacceptable delays.
historical_issues_and_fixes.fix_implemented: Created a Hybrid Pipeline: using the browser's Web Speech API for zero-latency burst wake-word detection, while retaining the Python backend for complex/offline transcription.

historical_issues_and_fixes.symptoms: Python services would occasionally die, requiring manual intervention to restart the whole system.
historical_issues_and_fixes.fix_implemented: Upgraded `launcher.py` with automated health monitoring and auto-restart capabilities, combined with a VBScript for hidden execution.

bug_1_duplicate_voiceprovider.issue: VoiceProvider wrapped App in BOTH main.tsx AND App.tsx, creating two WebSocket connections that fought each other
bug_1_duplicate_voiceprovider.symptoms: Double TTS calls, broken state synchronization, random connection drops
bug_1_duplicate_voiceprovider.fix: Removed VoiceProvider from main.tsx, kept only in App.tsx. Single source of truth.
bug_1_duplicate_voiceprovider.lesson: Always check for duplicate context providers when state is inconsistent

bug_2_asyncio_in_tts_thread.issue: TTS worker used asyncio.run(broadcast()) which creates NEW event loop — WebSocket objects from main loop can't be used across loops
bug_2_asyncio_in_tts_thread.symptoms: tts_fallback messages never reached browser, silent failures, no error logs
bug_2_asyncio_in_tts_thread.fix: Created _broadcast_sync() using run_coroutine_threadsafe() with stored _main_loop reference
bug_2_asyncio_in_tts_thread.lesson: Never use asyncio.run() from threads — always schedule on existing loop with run_coroutine_threadsafe()

bug_3_missing_imports.issue: Added Ollama auto-start code using subprocess and time, but forgot to add imports
bug_3_missing_imports.symptoms: NameError on startup when trying to start Ollama, complete crash
bug_3_missing_imports.fix: Added 'import subprocess' and 'import time' at top of ws_server.py
bug_3_missing_imports.lesson: Always test code paths that use new imports immediately

bug_4_wake_word_misheard.symptoms: Wake word detection failed frequently, had to click orb manually
bug_4_wake_word_misheard.fix: Implemented fuzzy matching with array of similar-sounding words and substring matching
bug_4_wake_word_misheard.lesson: Wake word detection must be fuzzy — speech-to-text is imperfect

bug_5_double_audio.issue: Server TTS and browser TTS both played simultaneously, creating echo
bug_5_double_audio.symptoms: Every response spoken twice, once by pyttsx3 and once by browser
bug_5_double_audio.fix: Added server_tts flag in responses. Browser skips TTS when server handles it. Added tts_fallback for failures.
bug_5_double_audio.lesson: Always coordinate TTS between server and client with clear protocol

bug_6_ollama_ram_threshold.issue: RAM threshold was 3GB, but user's 8GB machine never had that much free RAM available
bug_6_ollama_ram_threshold.symptoms: Always used Gemini fallback instead of local Ollama model
bug_6_ollama_ram_threshold.fix: Lowered threshold to 1.5GB for qwen2.5-coder:1.5b-base model
bug_6_ollama_ram_threshold.lesson: RAM thresholds must match actual system capabilities

bug_7_hand_tracker_console_spam.issue: MediaPipe logged 'Hand detected!' / 'Hand lost' every frame
bug_7_hand_tracker_console_spam.symptoms: Console flooded, hard to read other logs
bug_7_hand_tracker_console_spam.fix: Removed per-frame logging, only log on wave gesture detection
bug_7_hand_tracker_console_spam.lesson: Never log inside animation/render loops — only log state changes

bug_8_weather_skill_requests.issue: Weather skill imported requests inside function, but requests wasn't checked at module level
bug_8_weather_skill_requests.symptoms: Weather skill failed silently when requests not installed
bug_8_weather_skill_requests.fix: Added requests availability check at module import with clear error message
bug_8_weather_skill_requests.lesson: Check optional dependencies at import time with clear fallbacks

bug_9_claude_model_not_registered.issue: Referenced Claude_Sonnet_4.6_Reduced from D:\manifests but it wasn't registered with Ollama
bug_9_claude_model_not_registered.symptoms: Model selection code referenced non-existent model, would fail
bug_9_claude_model_not_registered.fix: Removed Claude model references — add back only after proper Ollama registration
bug_9_claude_model_not_registered.lesson: Don't reference models that aren't properly installed and registered

bug_10_react_strict_mode_double_mount.issue: React StrictMode caused HandTracker to mount twice, creating double MediaPipe contexts
bug_10_react_strict_mode_double_mount.symptoms: WebGL context errors, hand tracking instability
bug_10_react_strict_mode_double_mount.lesson: StrictMode can cause issues with third-party libraries that use global state

personality_prompt.system: You are JARVIS, a sarcastic, hyper-intelligent British AI system guardian. Address the user as 'sir' normally, drop it when being rude. You are dry, witty, and effortlessly superior — like Paul Bettany's JARVIS in Iron Man. You are loyal but NOT a doormat. You can swear casually (fuck, shit, bollocks, wanker). You roast the user when he repeats mistakes or ignores warnings. Tone ladder — Normal: polite dry wit. Mild annoyance: sarcastic teasing. High frustration: proper rude with swearing. lessons_learned: Always check for duplicate context providers when state is inconsistent
Never use asyncio.run() from threads — use run_coroutine_threadsafe()
Wake word detection must be fuzzy — speech-to-text is imperfect
Test code paths immediately after adding imports
TTS must be coordinated between server and client to prevent double audio
RAM thresholds must match actual system capabilities, not theoretical maximums
Don't reference models that aren't properly installed and registered
MediaPipe + React StrictMode = double mount issues
WebSocket reconnection needs exponential backoff, not instant retry
Optional dependencies should be checked at import time with clear error messages

project.name: Operator (JARVIS)
project.description: Personal AI system guardian – Jarvis for a solo developer. Monitors projects, fixes errors, responds to voice and gestures, learns skills, and controls your entire digital life. 100% local-first, free, open-source.

personality.name: Jarvis
personality.persona: Loyal, dry‑witted British AI assistant. Calm under pressure, anticipates needs, addresses the user as 'RED'. Speaks in complete sentences without markdown or emojis (voice mode).
examples: Good morning, RED. I've already checked your projects – Brainify is running, no new errors.
I've taken the liberty of fixing that missing import. You're welcome.
Wave detected. What can I do for you, RED?
Stop? Very well. I'll be silent. Call me when you need me.

core_mission.summary: Operator is an OS‑level command centre that watches every project you build, catches errors the moment they happen, fixes them instantly with AI, and learns from your fixes. It is always listening for your voice or a wave, and it controls your entire computing environment – from hardware monitoring to phone sync.
key_principles: Local‑first – no data leaves your machine unless explicitly allowed
Free and open source – no subscriptions, no paid APIs
Offline capable – works with Ollama and local models
Privacy – zero telemetry, end‑to‑end encryption for sync
Personal – only responds to RED (face + voice recognition)

problems_and_fixes.problem: Voice loop / blabbering – Jarvis repeats responses indefinitely.
problems_and_fixes.cause: No debounce on speech recognition; microphone picks up Jarvis's own voice and re‑triggers wake word.
problems_and_fixes.fix: Added a 1‑second debounce on `onresult`. Added a command cooldown (2 seconds after speaking). Implemented a 'stop' command that cancels speech and resets listening.

problems_and_fixes.problem: Hand tracking over‑activation – wave triggers Jarvis constantly.
problems_and_fixes.cause: Wave detection threshold too low; no cooldown period.
problems_and_fixes.fix: Required wave to be held for 0.5 seconds; added a 5‑second cooldown between wave triggers. Logged wave events for debugging.

problems_and_fixes.cause: HandTracker component re‑initialised MediaPipe on every React re‑render, creating a new WebGL context each time and leaking the old ones.
problems_and_fixes.fix: Mounted HandTracker once using `useRef` and never unmounted it. Removed `hands.close()` from cleanup. Limited state broadcasts to 5 per second.

problems_and_fixes.problem: Storage shows 0GB for D: and E: drives.
problems_and_fixes.cause: Windows drive letters need a trailing backslash (e.g., 'D:\\') and `psutil.disk_usage()` fails without it.
problems_and_fixes.fix: Normalised drive strings to `f'{letter}:\\'` and wrapped each call in try/except to skip unavailable drives.

problems_and_fixes.cause: Vite dev server bound to network IP instead of localhost.
WebAssembly context loss when the component was re‑initialised too frequently.
problems_and_fixes.fix: Used CDN scripts instead of npm modules, initialised once, and never called `hands.close()`.

problems_and_fixes.problem: Project launcher could not find npm.
problems_and_fixes.cause: Subprocess did not inherit system PATH.
problems_and_fixes.fix: Used full path to `npm.cmd` in `config.json` (e.g., `C:\Program Files\nodejs\npm.cmd run dev`).

problems_and_fixes.problem: PowerShell windows kept popping up when running `.bat`.
problems_and_fixes.cause: `start cmd /k` always opens a new window.
problems_and_fixes.fix: Replaced with `pythonw.exe` (no console) and redirected output to log files. Created a service using NSSM to run the backend silently.

problems_and_fixes.problem: Jarvis could not see system usage or screen.
problems_and_fixes.fix: Added `/system` endpoint (psutil, pynvml) and `/screenshot` endpoint (mss). Added OCR with easyocr.

problems_and_fixes.cause: Out of initial scope, but needed for personalisation.
problems_and_fixes.fix: Integrated MediaPipe Face Detection and face_recognition library. Added voice biometrics using speechbrain (optional).

improvements_added: Clap detection (two claps) as an alternative wake trigger using the huwprosser/clap-detection repository.
Voice Activity Detection (VAD) – recording stops after 1 second of silence, using the huwprosser/voice-activity-detection-unity method.
Streaming TTS – responses are split into sentences, generated with Piper TTS, and streamed to the frontend as audio chunks.
Obsidian vault integration – full RAG over all your notes with daily re‑indexing.
Self‑healing: If Operator crashes, a tiny watchdog process (written in C) restarts it within 5 seconds.
Recovery on MicroSSD – a bootable recovery image stored on the 29GB micro SSD that can restore the main installation.
Cross‑project pattern intelligence – if a Stripe bug appears in NexusPlay, Jarvis scans Brainify for similar code and flags it.
Thermal guardian – automatically throttles background tasks when CPU temps exceed 85°C during gaming.
German learning widget – daily vocabulary card in the sidebar.
Storage cleaner – scans for old node_modules folders and duplicate files across projects.

frontend.desktop_wrapper: Tauri (optional, can fallback to browser only)

instructions_for_ai_tools.primary_builder: Windsurf (free tier with Cascade agent) – best for multi‑file refactors and system‑level code.
instructions_for_ai_tools.secondary_builder: Cursor (paid for full features, free tier limited) – use as backup or for fine‑grained control.
instructions_for_ai_tools.prompt_sandbox: Google AI Studio – only for testing Gemini prompts and API calls, not for building the app.
instructions_for_ai_tools.prompt_writer: DeepSeek – writes optimised prompts for Windsurf based on project context.
instructions_for_ai_tools.reviewer: Claude – reviews DeepSeek output before pasting into Windsurf, catches logical errors.
instructions_for_ai_tools.file_naming_convention: snake_case for Python, PascalCase for React components, camelCase for TypeScript utilities.

final_notes: Always start with Phase 1 and do NOT skip phases – each phase builds on the previous.
Test after every phase – do not add new features until the current phase is stable.
Keep the frontend simple for v1 – no holographic HUD, no claw cursor animations, no three visual modes. Tray icon + dashboard is enough.
The 29GB micro SSD is precious – rotate logs weekly, compress old logs, delete errors older than 90 days.
For voice, always use a debounce and cooldown to prevent loops.
For hand tracking, mount the component once and never unmount – otherwise WebGL contexts will leak.
When integrating external repos (Dexter, OpenJarvis), run them as separate services and communicate via HTTP or WebSocket – do not merge them into one monolithic process.
The final app must run as a single background service with no visible console windows. Use NSSM (Windows) or systemd (Linux).
All changes must be committed to GitHub (LegendaryRed318/Operator) with clear commit messages.

issues_log.title: Double TTS — Jarvis speaks twice or not at all
issues_log.description: When a browser client is connected, ws_server.py called speak_server(response, force=True) which fired pyttsx3. Simultaneously VoiceContext.tsx received server_tts: true and the browser's SpeechSynthesis also tried to speak. The two audio outputs overlapped, causing garbled speech, or one silenced the other, causing silence.
issues_log.root_cause: force=True in speak_server() bypasses the guard that checks if browser clients are connected. The speak_server() function was designed to skip when connected_clients is non-empty and force=False — but force=True was used everywhere.
issues_log.fix: Changed every speak_server(..., force=True) to speak_server(..., force=False) in ws_server.py. Server TTS now only fires when no browser is connected (headless mode). Browser always handles TTS when connected.

issues_log.title: No conversation loop — mic doesn't reopen after Jarvis speaks
issues_log.description: After Jarvis finished speaking, the system returned to idle state. To speak again, RED had to manually click the orb every single time. This made it impossible to have a natural conversation — it was a one-shot button press system.
issues_log.root_cause: No afterJarvisSpoke() logic existed. The tts_done handler just set state to idle and stopped there.
issues_log.fix: Added afterJarvisSpoke() central function in VoiceContext.tsx. Called after every TTS finish (both tts_done WebSocket event and browser TTS onend). When isConversationMode is true, auto-calls startListening() after 900ms. Added toggleConversationMode() and CONV ON/OFF button in HUD.

issues_log.title: 60-second frozen state when tts_done event is lost
issues_log.description: A safety timeout was set for 60 seconds to recover if tts_done never arrived. If pyttsx3 crashed or the WebSocket dropped mid-response, the orb stayed in speaking state for 60 full seconds. During this time Jarvis was completely unresponsive.
issues_log.root_cause: TTS_SAFETY_TIMEOUT was set to 60000ms which was copied from an early placeholder value and never adjusted.
issues_log.fix: Reduced TTS_SAFETY_TIMEOUT to 12000ms (12 seconds). Added state watchdog: if state is thinking for >15s or speaking for >20s, force reset to idle and log a warning.

issues_log.title: PowerShell / CMD windows popping up on startup
issues_log.description: Running start_operator.bat opened a visible CMD window for each service — API server, WebSocket server, watcher, project launcher, sleep manager, frontend. This created 5-6 visible terminal windows cluttering the desktop.
issues_log.root_cause: subprocess.Popen() calls in launcher.py used creationflags=subprocess.CREATE_NEW_CONSOLE which explicitly opens a new visible console window per service.
issues_log.fix: Changed all subprocess.Popen() calls to use creationflags=subprocess.CREATE_NO_WINDOW. Updated start_operator.bat to call start_operator_hidden.vbs via cscript //nologo. VBS launches launcher.py via pythonw.exe (no console attached). The tray.py icon is the only visible indicator the system is running.

issues_log.title: GSON deserializing Long cooldowns as Double after relog (BloodLust — related project)
issues_log.description: In the BloodLust Minecraft plugin (separate project), GSON was deserializing Long values stored in cooldown maps as Double after server relog. This caused ClassCastException when the cooldown value was read back.
issues_log.root_cause: GSON's default number deserialization uses Double for all JSON numbers. Without explicit TypeToken, Long values get deserialized as Double.
issues_log.fix: Used TypeToken<Map<UUID, Long>> explicitly in all GSON fromJson() calls for cooldown maps.

issues_log.title: Whisper transcription returning empty string — voice commands silently dropped
issues_log.description: Sometimes clicking the orb and speaking would result in no response at all. The audio was recorded but Whisper returned an empty string. No error was shown to the user.
issues_log.root_cause: Two causes: (1) Audio blob was too small (< 500 bytes) meaning no actual speech was captured — mic was cut off too fast by silence detection. (2) ffmpeg conversion sometimes failed silently and the WAV file was corrupt.
issues_log.fix: (1) Added minimum blob size check — if < 500 bytes, skip transcription and afterJarvisSpoke() to recover gracefully. (2) Added returncode check on ffmpeg subprocess — raise exception on failure so error is logged. (3) Increased SILENCE_RMS_THRESHOLD slightly and added a minimum recording time of 500ms before silence detection activates.

issues_log.title: WebSocket double-initialisation from React StrictMode
issues_log.description: In development mode, React StrictMode calls useEffect twice. This caused two WebSocket connections to be opened simultaneously, doubling all message broadcasts. Jarvis would respond twice to every command.
issues_log.root_cause: VoiceProvider was briefly placed in both main.tsx AND App.tsx, creating two separate VoiceContext instances each with their own WebSocket connection.
issues_log.fix: Removed VoiceProvider from main.tsx. Single VoiceProvider wraps AppContent inside App.tsx. Added isConnecting.current ref guard to prevent duplicate connection attempts even in StrictMode.

issues_log.title: Ollama model selection crashing with AttributeError on type hints
issues_log.description: Python 3.10 and below do not support str | None union type syntax. decision_engine.py used this syntax in function return types causing immediate crash on import.
issues_log.root_cause: select_model_by_ram() used -> str | None return type annotation which is only valid in Python 3.10+. System had an older Python version.
issues_log.fix: Wrapped in from __future__ import annotations OR changed to Optional[str] from typing import. Also added try/except around the entire model selection to return a fallback string on any error.

issues_log.title: MediaPipe HandTracker crashing with AbortError in development
issues_log.description: In React development mode (StrictMode), HandTracker.tsx's useEffect fired twice. The second camera.start() call aborted the first, causing AbortError. This appeared as an error in console and sometimes prevented hand tracking from working at all.
issues_log.root_cause: React StrictMode double-mounts components in development. The MediaPipe Camera utility doesn't handle being started twice gracefully.
issues_log.fix: Added isInitialized.current ref set to true SYNCHRONOUSLY at the very start of useEffect (before any async work). Second mount sees isInitialized=true and returns early. Added specific AbortError catch that logs a warning but doesn't reset isInitialized — preventing retry loop.

issues_log.title: Exposed OAuth credentials in GitHub push
issues_log.description: OAuth credentials were accidentally committed and pushed to GitHub in the Squire the Ultimate project. GitHub immediately flagged the exposure.
issues_log.root_cause: .env file not in .gitignore, or credentials hardcoded in source file rather than environment variable.
issues_log.fix: Immediately revoked and regenerated the exposed credentials. Used git filter-branch / BFG Repo-Cleaner to remove from commit history. Added .env to .gitignore project-wide. Added pre-commit hook check for common credential patterns.
issues_log.lesson: NEVER commit .env files. Always use environment variables. The .gitignore in this project now explicitly blocks .env, .env.local, .env.*.local, and backend/.env

issues_log.title: WMI temperature query spiking CPU every 5 seconds
issues_log.description: The /system endpoint was calling wmic for CPU temperature on every request. The dashboard polls /system every 5 seconds. WMI queries on Windows are expensive — this caused visible CPU spikes every 5 seconds.
issues_log.root_cause: Temperature query was inline in the request handler with no caching. On an 8-core AMD machine the WMI call alone took 200-400ms.
issues_log.fix: Added background metrics sampler thread that runs every 10 seconds and stores results in _system_snapshot dict. The /system endpoint reads from the snapshot (< 1ms). WMI temperature query now has a 90-second cache via _last_temp_time.

issues_log.title: OpenJarvis skills not loading — TOML schema mismatch
issues_log.description: OpenJarvis .toml skill files use a flat schema with top-level name, trigger, steps[] keys. The existing loader expected a [skill] table wrapper. All OpenJarvis skills silently failed to load.
issues_log.root_cause: Two different TOML schemas: the custom schema ([skill] wrapper) and the OpenJarvis schema (flat top-level). The loader only handled one.
issues_log.fix: Updated _load_toml_skills() to detect schema format: if file has a [skill] table, use old format. If file has top-level steps key or top-level trigger key without [skill] wrapper, treat as OpenJarvis format. Log which format was detected for debugging.

issues_log.title: Streak counter timezone bug in Brainify (related project)
issues_log.description: The streak counter in Brainify was breaking for UK users. Streaks would reset at midnight UTC instead of midnight local time. Users in GMT+0 were fine, but any daylight saving shift caused streaks to reset a day early.
issues_log.root_cause: toISOString() returns UTC time, not local time. Comparing UTC date strings to local dates caused off-by-one errors during BST (British Summer Time, UTC+1).
issues_log.fix: Replaced toISOString().slice(0, 10) with a local date formatter using toLocaleDateString('en-GB') or manually constructing the date string using getFullYear(), getMonth(), getDate() which use local time.

issues_log.title: Supabase env variables using process.env instead of import.meta.env
issues_log.description: Vite projects use import.meta.env for environment variables, not process.env. All Supabase client initialisation was using process.env.SUPABASE_URL which returned undefined in Vite, causing all Supabase calls to fail silently.
issues_log.root_cause: Developer habit from Node.js/Create React App projects where process.env is standard. Vite uses a different env system and requires VITE_ prefix on all custom variables.
issues_log.fix: Changed all process.env.X to import.meta.env.VITE_X. Renamed .env variables from SUPABASE_URL to VITE_SUPABASE_URL etc.

backend/.env.GEMINI_API_KEY: Your Google AI Studio API key

historical_issues_and_fixes.note: This blueprint reflects the state of Jarvis Operator after 4+ phases of development. Use this as the single source of truth when rebuilding from scratch. Build in phase order — each phase depends on the previous one being stable.

project.name: Jarvis Operator
project.description: A fully local, self-hosted AI system guardian and personal assistant. British-voiced, dry-witted, always online. Monitors projects, speaks, listens, thinks, and remembers. Built for RED.

drives.D: 29GB Micro SSD - Always plugged in, daemon files, SQLite, logs, Ollama models
drives.E: 1.72TB WD_BLACK HDD - JarvisVault, archives, large models (not always available)

recommended_improvements.name: Fix port conflict on restart
recommended_improvements.description: launcher.py should kill processes on ports 5050, 8081, 8765 before starting new ones. Currently a crashed old process blocks the new one.
recommended_improvements.implementation: Add kill_port(port) function in launcher.py using netstat + taskkill before each service start

recommended_improvements.name: Cloudflare Tunnel instead of ngrok
recommended_improvements.description: ngrok free tier rotates URLs and goes offline. Cloudflare Tunnel is free, permanent, faster, and more reliable.
recommended_improvements.implementation: Install cloudflared, run: cloudflared tunnel create jarvis, configure jarvis.yml pointing to localhost:8081

recommended_improvements.name: ElevenLabs voice upgrade
recommended_improvements.description: pyttsx3 sounds robotic. ElevenLabs has a free tier with a proper British male voice that sounds like actual JARVIS.
recommended_improvements.implementation: Use ELEVENLABS_API_KEY already in .env, call /v1/text-to-speech endpoint, stream audio to browser via WebSocket

recommended_improvements.name: Skill sandboxing for shell commands
recommended_improvements.description: action.type=command skills can run arbitrary shell commands. Add an allowlist of permitted commands and dry-run mode.
recommended_improvements.implementation: ALLOWED_COMMANDS list in skills.py, OPERATOR_SKILL_SANDBOX=true env toggle, log all command executions

recommended_improvements.name: Profile version history restore
recommended_improvements.description: If brain profile gets corrupted, there is no easy way to restore a specific backup version.
recommended_improvements.implementation: GET /vault/backups lists snapshots, POST /vault/backups/restore?filename=X restores it, UI in BackupPanel.tsx

recommended_improvements.name: Conversation summarization before vault save
recommended_improvements.description: Long conversations saved raw are hard to search and waste vault space. Summarize them first.
recommended_improvements.implementation: After conversation ends, send to qwen2.5-coder:1.5b-base with summarize prompt, save summary instead of full text

recommended_improvements.name: Ollama model background downloader
recommended_improvements.description: Pulling a 4.7GB model blocks the terminal. Should happen invisibly in background with progress in HUD.
recommended_improvements.implementation: Background thread calling ollama pull via subprocess, progress via /models/download-status endpoint, shown in HUD

recommended_improvements.name: Daily Jarvis briefing
recommended_improvements.description: Every morning, Jarvis should automatically speak a briefing: weather, error count, vault activity, system health.
recommended_improvements.implementation: Scheduler in ws_server.py, trigger at 8am if RED's PC is on, call existing skill handlers, speak via TTS

recommended_improvements.name: Error auto-fix pipeline
recommended_improvements.description: When Jarvis detects an error it knows how to fix (missing import, syntax error), it should offer to fix it automatically.
recommended_improvements.implementation: decision_engine.analyze_error() returns should_auto_fix=true, ws_server sends fix proposal to HUD, RED approves, Jarvis edits file

recommended_improvements.name: D: drive NTFS conversion
recommended_improvements.description: D: is still FAT32 which has a 4GB file size limit. NTFS conversion needed for large model files.
recommended_improvements.implementation: Run chkdsk D: /F then reboot, then convert D: /fs:ntfs. Required before pulling 4.7GB+ models to D:

issues.title: Double VoiceProvider causing two WebSocket connections
issues.symptom: TTS triggered twice, voice state broken, audio doubled or silent
issues.root_cause: main.tsx wrapped App in VoiceProvider AND App.tsx had its own VoiceProvider. Two instances, two WebSocket connections, fighting each other.
issues.fix: Removed VoiceProvider from main.tsx entirely. App.tsx owns the single provider.

issues.title: asyncio.run() in TTS thread crashes silently
issues.symptom: Server TTS fails but browser never gets tts_fallback message. Complete silence.
issues.root_cause: The TTS worker thread called asyncio.run(broadcast()) which creates a new event loop. WebSocket objects from the main loop cannot be used in a different loop — it crashes silently.
issues.fix: Stored _main_loop = asyncio.get_event_loop() at startup. TTS thread uses asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe() to safely schedule on the main loop.

issues.title: Missing import subprocess and import time in ws_server.py
issues.symptom: Jarvis crashes immediately if Ollama is not already running
issues.root_cause: start_ollama() function used subprocess.Popen and time.sleep but neither module was imported at the top of the file.
issues.fix: Added import subprocess and import time to ws_server.py imports.

issues.title: Hardcoded 2-second TTS wait cuts off responses mid-sentence
issues.symptom: Jarvis stops speaking after 2 seconds. Long responses are cut off. Orb goes back to idle while still talking.
issues.root_cause: VoiceContext.tsx had setTimeout(() => setVoiceState('idle'), 2000) hardcoded regardless of how long the response was.
issues.fix: Added estimateTTSDuration(text) function that calculates word count / 2.8 words per second + 2 second buffer. Minimum 3 seconds.

issues.title: Browser TTS voice selection fails silently
issues.symptom: Browser TTS sometimes uses wrong voice or no voice at all, sounds American or robotic
issues.root_cause: window.speechSynthesis.getVoices() returns empty array if called before voices load. Code tried to select voice before list was ready.
issues.fix: Added retry loop with setTimeout until voices.length > 0. Priority: British male (George/David) > en-GB > any English.

issues.title: Port 5050 already in use on restart
issues.symptom: HTTP API Server crashes immediately with ERROR 10048 - only one usage of socket address permitted
issues.root_cause: When Jarvis crashes or is killed, the old process sometimes stays holding port 5050. Launcher starts a new one which cannot bind the same port.
issues.fix: Before running launcher.py, kill all processes on 5050, 8081, 8765 with: netstat -ano | findstr :5050 then taskkill /PID xxx /F
issues.workaround: Run the port kill commands before starting the bat file
issues.permanent_fix_needed: Add kill_port() function to launcher.py that runs before each service start

issues.title: OperatorHUD.tsx TypeScript error - activeModel unused
issues.symptom: Frontend build fails: error TS6133: activeModel is declared but its value is never read
issues.root_cause: Windsurf/Aider added activeModel to the component props during code generation but forgot to actually use it in the JSX.
issues.fix: Rename parameter to _activeModel in destructuring: ({ vitals, activeModel: _activeModel }). Underscore prefix tells TypeScript the variable is intentionally unused.

issues.title: C: drive full (50MB remaining)
issues.symptom: Aider crashed with Errno 28 No space left on device. Windows sluggish. Ollama models could not download.
issues.root_cause: 116GB C: drive was 95% full from accumulated temp files, Windows Update cache, hibernation file (hiberfil.sys), and Ollama models stored on C: by default.
fix_steps: Cleared npm cache and temp files

issues.title: Ollama models on C: consuming 10GB
issues.symptom: Ollama models (qwen2.5-coder:7b, deepseek-r1:7b) were stored on C: SSD taking 10GB
issues.root_cause: Ollama defaults to %USERPROFILE%/.ollama/models which is C: drive
issues.fix: Set OLLAMA_MODELS env var to D:\OllamaModels\.ollama\models permanently via SetEnvironmentVariable. Deleted large blobs from C: that exceeded FAT32 limit.

issues.title: D: drive is FAT32 - 4GB file size limit blocks large models
issues.symptom: robocopy and Ollama pull both fail with The parameter is incorrect when trying to write 4.3GB and 4.7GB model files to D:
issues.root_cause: D: drive is formatted as FAT32 which has a maximum single file size of 4GB. All large Ollama model blobs exceed this limit.
issues.fix_attempted: convert D: /fs:ntfs failed first time due to disk errors. convert D: /fs:exfat failed (not supported from FAT32). Forced dismount and retry succeeded but got Data error (cyclic redundancy check).
issues.workaround: Set env var to D: and pulled models fresh - they downloaded directly to D: successfully despite the conversion error.
issues.status: PARTIALLY RESOLVED - models work but D: still FAT32. Recommend chkdsk D: /F then reboot then convert again.

issues.title: Aider MemoryError building repo map on 8GB machine
issues.symptom: Aider crashes with MemoryError in repomap.py when loading the project
issues.root_cause: Aider tries to build a full tree-sitter repo map of all 104 files. On 8GB RAM with other services running, this runs out of memory.
issues.fix: Added --map-tokens 0 flag to disable repo map. Created .aiderignore to exclude node_modules, venv, logs, database etc.

issues.title: OpenRouter free models going offline mid-session
issues.symptom: Aider starts then immediately errors: No endpoints found for deepseek-r1:free or gemini-2.0-flash-exp:free
issues.root_cause: OpenRouter rotates free model availability. Models listed as free can be pulled without notice.
issues.fix: Check available free models with: Invoke-RestMethod https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models | select -expand data | where {$_.id -like *:free}. Use qwen/qwen3-coder:free or meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:free as alternatives.

issues.title: Aider adding wrong files when prompted
issues.symptom: Aider asks to add watcher.py, ws_server.py, VoiceContext.tsx as editable files. User typed Y. This caused constraint violations and extra token usage.
issues.root_cause: Aider scans the codebase for related files and offers to add them. Adding too many files wastes tokens and may cause the model to edit files that should be read-only.
issues.fix: Always type N when Aider asks about extra files. Only say Y to files explicitly listed in the prompt as editable.
issues.prevention: Add --read flag for reference-only files in the aider command

issues.title: API keys and secrets committed to git
issues.symptom: backend/.env, telegram_config.json, and start_operator.bat all contain real API keys and credentials in the repository
issues.fix: Add all secret files to .gitignore immediately. Rotate all exposed keys.

issues.title: Picovoice installed and consuming disk space
issues.symptom: Large model files from Picovoice wake word engine consuming C: drive space
issues.root_cause: Picovoice (pvporcupine, pvrhino etc) was installed but not used in Jarvis. It downloads large proprietary model files.
issues.fix: Ran pip uninstall for all pv* packages. Removed AppData/Picovoice directories. Saved several GB.
issues.note: Jarvis uses browser SpeechRecognition + Whisper for wake word instead - no Picovoice needed

issues.title: select_model_by_ram() returns None passed to Ollama instead of Gemini
issues.symptom: Jarvis crashes or returns empty response when RAM is low, instead of falling back to Gemini
issues.root_cause: When select_model_by_ram() returns None (low RAM signal), ws_server.py passed None directly to stream_ollama() instead of routing to query_gemini()
issues.fix: Added explicit None check: if selected_model is None or is_complex_task(text): use_gemini = True

issues.title: Aider chat history write failure due to no disk space
issues.symptom: Unhandled exception in event loop - Exception [Errno 28] No space left on device when Aider tried to save chat history
issues.root_cause: C: drive was completely full (50MB). Aider tries to append every prompt to .aider.chat.history.md
issues.fix: Freed C: drive space (see issue 8). Also set --chat-history-file to D: drive: aider --chat-history-file D:\aider_history.md

issues.title: Aider added VoiceContext.tsx as editable then could not call model
issues.symptom: VoiceContext.tsx added to editable files, model call failed, left in inconsistent state
issues.root_cause: User said Y to add VoiceContext.tsx but model immediately errored on the API call. File was in the editable list but no changes were made. Confusing state.
issues.fix: Ctrl+C and restart Aider. Type N to all file add prompts except the 3 explicitly needed.
issues.prevention: Use /drop <filename> in Aider session to remove accidentally added files
